$comment: Shadow Banking Evidence Profile v1. This is a PROFILE (a binding over existing regulation), not a new ontology. It enumerates the evidence a single US consumer/commercial CREDIT decision record must contain to be examiner-auditable, maps each requirement to its regulatory hook (Reg B / FCRA / SR 26-2), and says where in a Shadow evidence bundle it is carried. The conformance checker (lib/enforce-banking-profile.js, `shadow-verify --profile banking-v1`) is the enforced artifact: no published standard owns the 'is this credit decision auditable?' pass/fail gate. Field-list precedent exists at the MODEL level (Model Cards, IBM FactSheets) and as generic provenance (W3C PROV); none is a per-decision, credit-regulation-mapped, machine-checkable profile. A generic 'decision event schema' concept is emerging in academia, so first-mover on the credit-specific, checker-enforced version is the defensible position — the differentiation is the regulation mapping + the conformance gate, not the schema.
applies_to: single regulated consumer/commercial credit decision (approve / deny / counteroffer / incomplete)

levels.required: must be present for any bundle to conform to banking-v1
levels.required_on_adverse: must be present when the decision is adverse (deny / counteroffer / incomplete)
levels.recommended: expected for a complete examiner packet; absence is a warning, not a failure

note_on_checking: Bundle payloads are content-addressed (payload_hash, not inline), so the checker verifies STRUCTURAL presence from the bundle alone (event types, header fields, signatures, extensions). Field-VALUE checks (e.g. reason codes actually AA-mapped) run only when the caller supplies the payloads map. Structural PASS means the evidence slot exists and is tamper-evident; it does not certify the decision was correct.

fields.label: Decision outcome + decision id
reg_hooks: Reg B 12 CFR 1002.9(a) (notification of action taken)
fields.provided_by: a model_output event; header.session_id as the decision id

fields.label: Policy / config / schema version in force
Input data pull + data-as-of timestamp
fields.provided_by: a tool_call or tool_result event (the data pull)

fields.label: Principal reason codes (<=4), specific + accurate, AA-mapped
fields.provided_by: reason_codes in a model_output / tool_result payload (value-level: requires payloads)

fields.label: Reason-code dictionary version, bound into the signature
fields.provided_by: dictionary_hash bound in an event extension, resolved against the reason-code-dictionary registry

fields.label: Source citations for the factors actually considered
fields.provided_by: citation_registry binding / citations in a model_output payload

fields.label: Retention / redaction status
reg_hooks: Reg B 12 CFR 1002.12 (25-month retention; 12 for business credit)
fields.provided_by: retention flag in header.extensions or a session_end payload; GDPR-erasure via payload_ref nulling

fields.label: Reviewer interaction detail (approved / modified / rejected + duration + override rationale)
fields.$comment: Deepens human_review from a binary approved-bool into the auditable record of WHAT the reviewer did: approved/modified/rejected, how long they spent, and — when they OVERRODE the model (modified/rejected) — WHY (a required non-empty override_rationale). Borrowed from the medical CAAT audit-trail spec (Frontiers in Digital Health 2026), whose minimum record set requires clinician-interaction metadata; the same question a bank examiner asks — did a human exercise independent judgment, or rubber-stamp the model? Recommended (not every bundle carries it yet); when the human_approval payload is supplied, the gate VERIFIES the structure rather than assuming it.
fields.provided_by: a reviewer_interaction object in the human_approval event payload

fields.label: Document source-map (each cited figure → page/region in a hash-bound source document)
fields.$comment: Complements source_citations (text-level, CFPB 2022-03) with document-level, post-hoc-swap-evident binding: a Document Source-Map (spec/document-source-map-v1.json) ties each extracted figure to a page+region in the ORIGINATION document, and its document_hash makes a later file swap fail verification. Recommended, not required — many decisions are not document-derived.
reg_hooks: FCRA accuracy of information relied upon
examiner ability to trace a cited figure to its origination document
fields.provided_by: a Document Source-Map bound via source_map_hash + document_hash in header.extensions or an event; supply the map itself in payloads to have the gate VERIFY it (not just presence)
